content: Google Unveils Gemini 2.5 Ultra: Frontier Reasoning and Multimodal Leap Reshapes LLM Leaderboards ... Home/ News/ Google Unveils Gemini 2.5 Ultra: Frontier ... Google Unveils Gemini 2.5 Ultra: Frontier Reasoning and Multimodal Leap Reshapes LLM Leaderboards ... On April 21, 2026, Google DeepMind formally announced the release of Gemini 2.5 Ultra, its most capable frontier model to date, available initially through Google AI Studio and Vertex AI API for enterprise and developer tiers. The release follows weeks of leaked internal benchmark scores circulating on AI tracking communities and represents Google's most direct challenge yet to OpenAI's o3-series and Anthropic's Claude 4 Opus in the reasoning-heavy segment of the market. The announcement was made via the official Google DeepMind blog and simultaneously communicated through a technical ... - Chain-of-thought reasoning backbone: A dedicated reasoning trace module similar in spirit to OpenAI's o-series, ... | MMLU-Pro (5-shot) | 93.| OpenAI o3 | 91.| | MATH-500 | 97.| OpenAI o3 | 96.| ... - OpenAI: A brief post on the OpenAI blog acknowledged the release and stated that an updated evaluation of o3-pro and a forthcoming o4 model would be shared in the coming weeks. No specific timeline was given. ... Gemini 2.5 Ultra's release consolidates Google DeepMind's position at the frontier as of April 2026 and intensifies pressure on OpenAI, Anthropic, and Meta to accelerate their release cadences. The next 30–60 days will likely see formal responses from at least OpenAI (o4) and Anthropic (Claude 4.5 or equivalent). Elon Musk originally indicated a Q1 2026 launch date for xAI’s next flagship model, but that window has passed without a release — the prediction market Polymarket gives only a probability that Grok 5 ships by June 30, 2026. xAI’s official communications now point to Q2 2026 as the most likely window for Grok 5’s release, with the Colossus 2 supercluster in Memphis, Tennessee expanded ... title: Google Launches AI Browser 'Gemini in Chrome' in South Korea, Accelerating Shift from 'Search' to 'Execution' Paradigm — BigGo Finance
summary: Google Launches AI Browser 'Gemini in Chrome' in South Korea, Accelerating Shift from 'Search' to 'Execution' Paradigm — BigGo Finance Google Launches AI Browser 'Gemini in Chrome' in South Korea, Accelerating Shift from 'Search' to 'Execution' Paradigm Google has launched 'Gemini in Chrome,' it
content: Google Launches AI Browser 'Gemini in Chrome' in South Korea, Accelerating Shift from 'Search' to 'Execution' Paradigm — ... Google Launches AI Browser 'Gemini in Chrome' in South Korea, Accelerating Shift from 'Search' to 'Execution' ... Google has launched 'Gemini in Chrome,' its AI model integrated directly into the Chrome browser, in South Korea and other Asian markets. This service allows users to handle various tasks—such as sending emails via Gmail or summarizing YouTube videos—within a single tab, signifying a paradigm shift from information "search" to task "execution." In ... players like OpenAI and Perplexity also joining the AI browser competition, the landscape of the global search market ... Global tech giant Google has made a decisive move to transform the search paradigm through artificial intelligence (AI). On the 21st, Google officially launched its 'Gemini in Chrome' service—which directly integrates its 'Gemini' AI model into the Chrome browser—in the Asia-Pacific (APAC) region, including South Korea. This is interpreted as a strategic move to reassert its dominance in the search market, which has been challenged by the rise of generative AI like ChatGPT, and to more deeply embed users within the Google ecosystem. Gemini in Chrome allows users to instantly invoke and utilize the Gemini AI from a side panel next to their current screen without opening a new webpage. First introduced in the United States in September last year, the service has gradually expanded its availability and has now landed in key Asian markets like South Korea, Japan, Singapore, and ... The service's greatest strength is its seamless integration with Google's vast service ecosystem. Without leaving the ... of a YouTube video, or checking Google Calendar to make optimal plans. It also includes Google's image-generation AI 'Imagen 2,' providing features like converting images displayed in the browser directly within the chat window. Sharmeen Desilva, Director of Product Management for Google Chrome, explained, "The goal of this feature is to complete ... minutes," emphasizing user convenience and productivity gains. This signifies an evolution beyond simply finding information towards becoming an 'AI agent' that executes tasks on behalf of the user. title: Amazon doubles down on AI with $5bn Anthropic bet
summary: Amazon doubles down on AI with $5bn Anthropic bet | AGBI Amazon doubles down on AI with $5bn Anthropic bet April 21, 2026, 3:34 PM Reuters/Bhawika Chhabra Anthropic's CEO Dario Amodei at February's AI Impact Summit in New Delhi, India - Deal could expand to $20bn - $350 billion valuation - Qa
content: Amazon doubles down on AI with $5bn Anthropic bet | AGBI Amazon doubles down on AI with $5bn Anthropic bet April 21, 2026, 3:34 PM Reuters/Bhawika Chhabra Anthropic's CEO Dario Amodei at February's AI Impact Summit in New Delhi, India - Deal could expand to $20bn - $350 billion valuation - Qatar and Abu Dhabi both investors Amazon is committing a further $5 billion to artificial intelligence company Anthropic, deepening its bet on a leading developer in the AI race as Gulf sovereign investors continue to back the ... The deal, which could expand to as much as $20 billion over time, was struck at a $350 billion valuation and reinforces a partnership centred on cloud computing and advanced chips. The latest move builds on existing Gulf involvement in the lab behind the Claude AI programme. The Qatar Investment Authority was a significant participant in a $13 billion funding round ... billion](https://www.agbi.com/ai/2026/02/mgx-close-to-joining-20bn-anthropic-fundraising/). Anthropic plans to spend more than $100 billion over the next decade on Amazon’s cloud infrastructure, underlining the scale of demand for computing power as companies race to build more advanced models. The AI lab will receive up to about 5 gigawatts of capacity over that period, with a significant share expected to come ... them](https://www.agbi.com/analysis/infrastructure/2026/01/off-grid-gas-plants-could-power-gulf-data-centres/)– is increasingly seen as critical to economic competitiveness and geopolitical influence. Anthropic, founded by former employees from rival AI company OpenAI, is best known for its Claude chatbot and AI-powered coding assistant, which are used by more than 100,000 customers through Amazon Web Services. The tie-up strengthens Amazon’s position in the AI infrastructure race, with its in-house Trainium chips competing against rival systems. title: Amazon’s $5B Anthropic bet is really about compute, not just cash
summary: Amazon’s $5B Anthropic bet is really about compute, not just cash | InfoWorld Amazon’s $5B Anthropic bet is really about compute, not just cash news Apr 21, 20265 mins The investment underscores a shift from pure funding to securing long-term compute, as Anthropic looks to ease capacity con
content: Amazon’s $5B Anthropic bet is really about compute, not just cash news Apr 21, 20265 mins The investment underscores a shift from pure funding to securing long-term compute, as Anthropic looks to ease ... Amazon on Monday said it was investing an additional $5 billion in Anthropic, a move that analysts say is aimed as much ... As part of the deal, Anthropic will lock in up to 5 gigawatts of compute capacity across AWS’s Trainium chips, including the new Trainium 3 and upcoming Trainium 4, the companies said in a joint statement. ... “Over time, the expanded capacity will let Anthropic support more users at once, build bigger models, and reduce these ... The analyst was referring to Anthropic’s move to throttle usage across its Claude subscriptions, especially during peak demand hours, which also coincided with other concerns, such as complaints of degradation in Claude’s reasoning performance, which is a cluster of nearly half a million chips, to train and run its models.The agreement between Amazon and Anthropic also includes an expansion of inference capacity in Asia and Europe, which Jain said should improve Claude’s speed and reliability globally. Anthropic will also have the option to buy future generations of Trainium as they become ... However, Anthropic isn’t alone when it comes to model providers trying to add compute capacity to train and run their ... For Anthropic, the Amazon deal comes with equally significant long-term obligations. The company has committed to ... For Amazon, the $5 billion investment builds on its earlier $8 billion bet on Anthropic and comes with the potential to commit up to an additional $20 billion tied to certain commercial milestones, which were not revealed. Anthropic is also looking beyond AWS. The company recently said it plans to add capacity using Google’s TPUs. These chips are ...
